game_name: Ogre Battle: The March of the Black Queen

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: income generation, tarot card effects, defense management
spell cards (Lovers, Moon, Fool) for tactical advantage
moral choices affect available units and endings
changing unit classes for tactical flexibility

typical_tension_triggers: liberated towns at risk of recapture
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: real-time multi-threaded tension with asynchronous threat management; tension escalates gradually as multiple fronts develop simultaneously rather than discrete waves

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: hidden towns and temples revealed only by moving units over specific map locations; hidden treasure caches similarly disclosed through exploration
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: revisiting completed stages allows hidden/neutral monster encounters; multiple hidden stages accessible through specific conditions during main campaign

difficulty_curve.early_game: introductory with tarot character customization determining starting unit composition and abilities; squad mechanics learned through initial deployments
difficulty_curve.mid_game: difficulty escalates through expanded theater with multiple simultaneous fronts requiring squad management, town defense, and resource allocation across larger maps
difficulty_curve.late_game: complex alignment/reputation system effects become critical; availability of rare units and hidden stages dependent on moral choices; boss encounters increase in difficulty
notable_spikes: first engagement with significant enemy forces
stages requiring defense of multiple settlements simultaneously
hidden stage access requiring specific reputation/alignment thresholds

notable_level_patterns.description: stages feature multiple towns/temples requiring simultaneous defense while player conducts offensive operations; enemy AI continuously attempts recapture of liberated settlements
time passing mechanics create constant maintenance cost drain; units with time-dependent abilities (vampires, werewolves) shift tactical validity based on cycle state

player decisions during town liberation (which units to deploy, which cities to prioritize) cumulatively shape reputation/alignment affecting available units and story branching; 13 different endings possible
notable_level_patterns.example_level: all stages contribute to ending determination

notable_level_patterns.description: certain stages and encounters locked behind alignment/reputation thresholds; exploration of completed stages may reveal new encounters; easter egg stages accessible only through specific play conditions
notable_level_patterns.example_level: hidden stages (4) plus easter egg stage
